food_subgroup: Korean lunchbox meal with marinated beef short ribs, rice, dumplings, and banchan side dishes

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for the composite Korean dosirak lunchbox meal. FOODON_00002473 (beef short rib food product) is used as the closest parent anchor for the primary protein component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (Beef, ribs) is used as the closest parent for the primary protein component. No composite Korean dosirak meal code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept exists for Korean dosirak lunchbox meal with kalbi.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard dosirak lunchbox set comprising approximately 150 g marinated grilled beef kalbi, 200 g steamed white rice, 80 g pork and chive mandu dumplings, and approximately 120 g of two banchan side dishes, totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beef, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0 inch fat, all grades, cooked, braised (NDB 23248); supplemented with USDA SR Legacy — Beef, short ribs, cooked; rice, white, cooked; pork and vegetable dumplings (estimated composite)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for the composite Beef Kalbi Dosirak meal. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite: approximately 27% marinated grilled beef short ribs (USDA FDC NDB 23248 and SR Legacy beef short rib cooked values), 36% steamed white rice (USDA SR Legacy 20045), 15% pork and chive mandu dumplings (USDA FDC closest match: pork and vegetable dumplings), and 22% mixed banchan (estimated from USDA SR Legacy kimchi, spinach namul, and similar Korean side dishes). Marinade contribution (soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil) is incorporated into the beef component estimate. All per-100g values reflect the blended composite at the stated serving proportions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for white rice (GI 64) and mixed Korean meal analogy
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beef Kalbi Dosirak as a composite meal. GI is estimated at 56 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate component being steamed white rice (GI 64 per University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). The protein and fat content from beef kalbi and mandu substantially blunt the postprandial glycaemic response relative to plain rice, consistent with mixed-meal GI attenuation principles documented in peer-reviewed literature (Wolever TMS et al., Am J Clin Nutr). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 112.75) / 100 ≈ 63.1, rounded to 63.0. Serving carbohydrate: 20.5 g per 100g × 550g serving = 112.75g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce marinade and banchan side dishes
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Beef Kalbi Dosirak due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 480 mg per 100g (approximately 2,640 mg per full serving), which substantially exceeds the recommended single-meal sodium ceiling of 600–800 mg for hypertensive patients; requesting reduced-sauce preparation and limiting banchan intake can partially mitigate sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index driven by white rice, partially attenuated by protein and fat from beef kalbi and mandu
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Beef Kalbi Dosirak with caution given its high glycemic load per serving (approximately 63), primarily from the white rice component; portion control of the rice, pairing with low-GI banchan such as kimchi, and monitoring postprandial blood glucose are recommended strategies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef short ribs and moderate cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of Beef Kalbi Dosirak due to its saturated fat content (approximately 2.9 g per 100g, approximately 16 g per serving) and cholesterol (approximately 38 mg per 100g), which may contribute to LDL elevation; choosing leaner cuts, reducing marinade sugar, and limiting portion size are advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Kalbi Dosirak (소갈비 도시락) is a Korean lunchbox meal centred on beef kalbi — boneless short ribs marinated in a traditional sauce of soy sauce, garlic, sesame oil, sugar, and grated Asian pear or apple, then grilled to caramelised tenderness. The dosirak format presents the kalbi alongside steamed white rice, crispy pan-fried pork and chive mandu dumplings, and two banchan (Korean side dishes) such as kimchi, spinach namul, or pickled vegetables, all arranged in a compartmentalised box. The beef is characteristically sweet-savoury with a smoky char, tender and juicy from the enzymatic tenderising action of the fruit in the marinade. As a composite mixed meal, it provides a substantial macronutrient profile: approximately 195 kcal per 100g, with 11.5g protein, 7.8g fat, and 20.5g carbohydrate. The high sodium content (approximately 480 mg per 100g) from the soy-based marinade and banchan is a key clinical concern for hypertensive individuals. The glycaemic index is estimated at 56 (medium) due to the white rice base, though the protein and fat from the beef and mandu attenuate the postprandial glycaemic response compared to plain rice. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten (soy sauce, mandu wrappers) or sesame. In Singapore, this dish is served at Korean restaurants such as Ajumma's and represents a popular lunch or dinner option combining the flavours of Korean barbecue with the convenience of a bento-style meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in beef kalbi dosirak?
What is in a Korean lunchbox?
Is galbi dosirak high in sodium?

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN) contains a direct entry for Beef Kalbi Dosirak as a composite meal. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FDC and SR Legacy entries for the individual components (beef short ribs cooked, white rice cooked, pork and vegetable dumplings, and Korean banchan analogues). GI is an analogous estimate based on the white rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database with mixed-meal attenuation applied. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component sources for this composite meal. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component sources and carry moderate uncertainty due to the composite estimation method. 3. The restaurant-specific product (Ajumma's Funan, Singapore) may differ from the estimated composite in marinade sugar content, portion size, and banchan composition; a direct laboratory analysis would improve confidence to high. 4. Halal status is listed as false as the dish contains pork mandu; the beef kalbi component alone may be halal-certifiable but the composite meal as described is not confirmed halal. 5. GI is estimated at 56 (medium) using analogous white rice GI (64, University of Sydney GI Database) with mixed-meal attenuation; this is an analogous_estimate and not a direct measurement. 6. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (56 × 112.75) / 100 = 63.1, rounded to 63.0, based on a 550g serving containing 20.5g carbohydrate per 100g.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Canonical food_name set to 'Beef Kalbi Dosirak' (removing promotional 'Signature' prefix). Korean local name added. Composite nutrient estimation performed from USDA FDC and SR Legacy component sources. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white rice entry with mixed-meal attenuation. All three health context conditions populated with specific clinical guidance. Ontology anchors set to closest_parent with gap notes. Aliases consistency verified between aliases_by_language and flat aliases. Sentinel value documentation added for all -1 fields.

Iteration 2: Addressed g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate failure. The note for 'vitamin_d_mcg' (which is -1) was refined to explicitly state 'not reported in the primary USDA component sources for this composite meal' to align more closely with the critical rule's example for documenting sentinel values.
